title: saussurea nipponica var. higo-montana
intro: Saussurea nipponica var. higo-montana, also known as Higo Mountain Saw-wort, is a plant species that belongs to the daisy family (Asteraceae) and is native to Japan. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever and inflammation, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds. The plant has been reported to have various health benefits, including reducing pain and swelling, and has been used to treat conditions such as arthritis and gout. In herbal preparations, Saussurea nipponica var. higo-montana is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties.
